name: Autovacuum Tuning and Maintenance
description: Configure and monitor PostgreSQL autovacuum to reclaim dead tuples, prevent table bloat, and avoid transaction ID wraparound.

steps.title: Check current autovacuum settings
steps.description: View the running autovacuum configuration parameters.
steps.command: psql -U postgres -c "SELECT name, setting, short_desc FROM pg_settings WHERE name LIKE 'autovacuum%' ORDER BY name;"

steps.title: Find tables needing vacuum
steps.description: Identify tables with the most dead tuples that are waiting for vacuum.
steps.command: psql -U postgres -d mydb -c "SELECT relname, n_live_tup, n_dead_tup, round(n_dead_tup::numeric / GREATEST(n_live_tup, 1) * 100, 2) AS dead_pct, last_autovacuum FROM pg_stat_user_tables WHERE n_dead_tup > 1000 ORDER BY n_dead_tup DESC LIMIT 10;"

steps.title: Run manual vacuum with verbose output
steps.description: Manually vacuum a specific table and see detailed statistics.
steps.tip: ANALYZE updates table statistics for the query planner. Always combine VACUUM with ANALYZE.

steps.title: Check table bloat
steps.description: Estimate wasted space from dead rows in frequently updated tables.
steps.command: psql -U postgres -d mydb -c "SELECT relname, pg_size_pretty(pg_total_relation_size(relid)) AS total_size, n_dead_tup, last_autovacuum, last_autoanalyze FROM pg_stat_user_tables ORDER BY pg_total_relation_size(relid) DESC LIMIT 10;"

steps.title: Tune autovacuum for a heavy-write table
steps.description: Override autovacuum settings at the table level for high-throughput tables.
steps.command: psql -U postgres -d mydb -c "ALTER TABLE orders SET (autovacuum_vacuum_scale_factor = 0.05, autovacuum_analyze_scale_factor = 0.02, autovacuum_vacuum_cost_delay = 10);"
steps.tip: Lower scale factors trigger vacuum sooner. Default is 0.2 (20% dead tuples). Set 0.05 for active tables.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# PostgreSQL Vacuum Maintenance Report
set -euo pipefail

DB="mydb"
USER="postgres"

echo "=== Autovacuum Configuration ==="
psql -U "$USER" -c "
SELECT name, setting
FROM pg_settings
WHERE name LIKE 'autovacuum%'
ORDER BY name;
"

echo ""
echo "=== Tables with most dead tuples ==="
psql -U "$USER" -d "$DB" -c "
SELECT relname AS table_name,
       n_live_tup AS live_rows,
       n_dead_tup AS dead_rows,
       round(n_dead_tup::numeric / GREATEST(n_live_tup, 1) * 100, 2) AS dead_pct,
       pg_size_pretty(pg_total_relation_size(relid)) AS total_size,
       last_autovacuum,
       last_autoanalyze
FROM pg_stat_user_tables
ORDER BY n_dead_tup DESC
LIMIT 15;
"

echo ""
echo "=== Transaction ID age (wraparound risk) ==="
psql -U "$USER" -d "$DB" -c "
SELECT relname,
       age(relfrozenxid) AS xid_age,
       pg_size_pretty(pg_total_relation_size(oid)) AS size
FROM pg_class
WHERE relkind = 'r' AND relnamespace = 'public'::regnamespace
ORDER BY age(relfrozenxid) DESC
LIMIT 10;
"

echo ""
echo "=== Currently running vacuums ==="
psql -U "$USER" -c "
SELECT pid, datname, query, state, now() - query_start AS duration
FROM pg_stat_activity
WHERE query LIKE '%VACUUM%' OR query LIKE '%vacuum%';
"

faq.question: What is transaction ID wraparound and why should I care?
faq.answer: PostgreSQL uses 32-bit transaction IDs that wrap around after ~2 billion transactions. If a table is not vacuumed before the threshold, PostgreSQL shuts down to prevent data corruption. Monitor age(relfrozenxid) and ensure autovacuum runs regularly.

faq.question: Should I run VACUUM FULL?
faq.answer: Rarely. VACUUM FULL rewrites the entire table and reclaims disk space but requires an exclusive lock that blocks all reads and writes. Use regular VACUUM for routine maintenance. Reserve VACUUM FULL for one-time space reclamation during maintenance windows.

faq.question: How do I know if autovacuum is keeping up?
faq.answer: Check pg_stat_user_tables for tables where n_dead_tup keeps growing and last_autovacuum is old. If dead tuple counts grow faster than autovacuum cleans them, increase autovacuum_max_workers or lower cost_delay.
